title: LedgerWise — Full Analysis Report
markdown:
# LedgerWise — Comprehensive Analysis Report

*Generated 2026-04-03 by Luna*

---

## Table of Contents
1. [Current State Assessment](#current-state)
2. [Security Issues (P0)](#security)
3. [Performance Issues (P1)](#performance)
4. [Feature Gap Analysis](#feature-gaps)
5. [AI Integration Roadmap](#ai-roadmap)
6. [UI/UX Redesign Guide](#ui-ux)
7. [Strategic Positioning](#positioning)
8. [Recommended Development Phases](#phases)

---

## 1. Current State Assessment {#current-state}

### What's Fully Implemented

The codebase is surprisingly complete for v0.1. **All 18 server files and 11 client views have real, working logic** — not stubs.

**Server Layer (Google Apps Script):**

| Service | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Config.gs | ✅ Complete | Centralized sheet names, column indices, account types |
| DAL.gs | ✅ Complete | All CRUD ops, pagination, lookup helpers, date range queries |
| Cache.gs | ✅ Complete | CacheService wrapper with TTL and invalidation |
| Auth.gs | ✅ Complete | RBAC (admin/accountant/viewer) with role hierarchy |
| ApiRouter.gs | ✅ Complete | 35+ API methods, auth gating per method |
| AccountsService.gs | ✅ Complete | Full CRUD with caching, duplicate detection, referential integrity |
| TransactionService.gs | ✅ Complete | Simple + compound journal entries, double-entry validation |
| LedgerService.gs | ✅ Complete | Account balances, running balance ledger |
| BankService.gs | ✅ Complete | CSV import, auto-categorize (rules + AI), approve/batch/learn |
| MappingService.gs | ✅ Complete | Full CRUD, substring + regex matching, priority sorting |
| ProjectService.gs | ✅ Complete | Full CRUD with per-project P&L |
| ReportService.gs | ✅ Complete | P&L, Balance Sheet (with retained earnings), Trial Balance, Dashboard |
| AuditService.gs | ✅ Complete | All mutations logged |
| PlaidService.gs | ✅ Complete | Full OAuth flow, transaction sync, dedup, connection management |
| AIService.gs | ✅ Complete | Gemini 2.5 Flash, batching, caching, few-shot, validation, learn-from-approval |
| Utils.gs | ✅ Complete | UUID, date/number parsing, validation, API response helpers |
| Main.gs | ✅ Complete | Web app entry point with HTML templating |
| Test.gs | ⚠️ Placeholder | Only contains `testFetch()` for OAuth authorization |

**Client Layer (Alpine.js SPA):**

| View | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Dashboard | ✅ Complete | KPI cards, Chart.js 6-month trend, recent transactions, skeleton loading |
| Chart of Accounts | ✅ Complete | Full CRUD, grouped by type |
| General Ledger | ✅ Complete | Pagination, date/account/project/search filters |
| Transaction Entry | ✅ Complete | Simple + compound modes, balance validation, account/project dropdowns |
| Bank Import | �� Complete | CSV upload/parse, Plaid Link, categorize review with filter pills, confidence bars, learn checkbox, batch approve |
| Mapping Rules | ✅ Complete | CRUD, regex toggle, pattern testing |
| Projects | ✅ Complete | CRUD with project P&L |
| P&L Report | ✅ Complete | Date range selection, revenue/expense breakdown |
| Balance Sheet | ✅ Complete | As-of date, assets/liabilities/equity sections |
| Trial Balance | ✅ Complete | Date range, debit/credit totals, balance check |
| Settings | ✅ Complete | Plaid config, Gemini API key, auth sheet setup |

**Tech Stack:** Tailwind CSS (CDN), Alpine.js, Chart.js, Feather Icons, Plaid Link SDK, Inter font.

### Naming Inconsistency

The app is called **"SmartLedger"** in all UI code (Main.gs title, sidebar logo, loading screen, localStorage keys) but **"LedgerWise"** in package.json. Need to pick one name and unify.

### What's Not Implemented

- **Zero tests** — no test framework, no unit tests, no integration tests
- **Reconciliation, Cash Flow, BvA sheets** defined in CONFIG but no service code or views
- **TRANSACTIONS sheet** defined in config but unused (all logic uses GL sheet)

---

## 2. Security Issues (P0) {#security}

These must be fixed before any production use:

### 🔴 Critical

**1. Plaid access tokens stored in plain text spreadsheet cells**
- File: `PlaidService.gs` → `_storeConnection()` / `_getConnectionSheet()`
- Risk: Anyone with spreadsheet access can steal bank access tokens
- Fix: Move tokens to `PropertiesService.getScriptProperties()` (encrypted at rest). Store only non-sensitive metadata (institution name, account mask, last sync) in the sheet.

**2. Anonymous admin fallback**
- File: `Auth.gs:39-40`
- Risk: If `getActiveUser().getEmail()` returns empty (certain deployment modes), user gets full admin as 'anonymous'
- Fix: Require a valid Google email. Return `authorized: false` for empty emails.

**3. Formula injection**
- Risk: User-provided text (descriptions, memos) written directly to cells. A value like `=IMPORTRANGE(...)` could exfiltrate data.
- Fix: Prefix cell values with `'` or sanitize leading `=`, `+`, `-`, `@` characters in DAL write operations.

**4. Webapp access set to "ANYONE"**
- File: `appsscript.json`
- Risk: Combined with Phase 1 auth (no Authorized_Users sheet), anyone with the URL gets full admin access
- Fix: Ensure Authorized_Users sheet exists on first deploy. Add setup flow that creates it.

### 🟡 Moderate

**5. Gemini API key preview sent to client** (`AIService.gs:36`) — leaks partial key info

**6. ReDoS risk** — User-provided regex patterns in mapping rules executed without timeout/sandboxing

---

## 3. Performance Issues (P1) {#performance}

These will break at ~500+ GL rows:

**1. LedgerService.getAllBalances() — N full table scans**
- Calls `getAccountBalance()` per account, each reading ALL GL rows
- 50 accounts = 50 full scans of the GL sheet
- Fix: Single-pass accumulator that iterates GL once and distributes to account buckets

**2. PlaidService._isDuplicate() — O(n×m) during sync**
- For each new transaction, reads entire Bank_Raw sheet
- Fix: Load Bank_Raw once, build a `Set` of existing bank references

**3. getLatestGLYear() — uncached full scan**
- Reads ALL GL rows every call, called multiple times per session
- Fix: Cache the result

**4. approveHighConfidence() — sequential API calls**
- Client-side for-loop making individual `google.script.run` calls
- Fix: Extend server-side `batchApprove` to support learn flag, use single API call

**5. PlaidService._getConnectionSheet() bypasses DAL**
- Calls `SpreadsheetApp.openById()` directly instead of `DAL.getSpreadsheet()`
- Violates the architecture's own principle

**6. Cache.invalidatePrefix() is broken**
- Hardcoded key list can't enumerate date-ranged cache keys (pl_2026-01-01_2026-03-31)
- P&L/BS/TB caches with date-range keys are never explicitly invalidated on write

---

## 4. Feature Gap Analysis {#feature-gaps}

### Must-Have (table stakes — all competitors offer these)

| Feature | QBO | Xero | FreshBooks | Wave | Zoho | GnuCash | LedgerWise |
|---------|-----|------|-----------|------|------|---------|------------|
| Bank reconciliation workflow | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ Partial |
| Invoicing | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | Basic | ❌ Missing |
| Accounts Receivable aging | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ Missing |
| Accounts Payable / Bills | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | Partial | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ Missing |
| Recurring transactions | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | �� | ✅ | ❌ Missing |
| Cash Flow Statement | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ Missing |
| Receipt/document capture | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ Missing |
| Customer/vendor management | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ Missing |
| Sales tax tracking | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ Missing |

### High-Priority (most competitors offer)

- Budgeting / Budget vs Actuals
- Multi-currency support
- Custom/scheduled report delivery
- Dashboard improvements
- Mobile access

### Nice-to-Have (differentiation)

- AI auto-reconciliation
- Inventory tracking
- Time tracking
- Project profitability reports (extends existing Projects)
- Payroll integration
- Payment processing
- Client/vendor portal

---

## 5. AI Integration Roadmap {#ai-roadmap}

### Current AI State

AIService.gs is **fully functional** with Gemini 2.5 Flash:
- Batched categorization (25 txns/batch)
- Prompt construction with CoA context + few-shot examples
- Response validation (account code existence check)
- Result caching (MD5 keyed)
- Learn-from-approval (auto-creates mapping rules)

### Technical Foundation

- **Best model choice:** Gemini Flash (already in use) — cheapest, fastest, native to Google
- **Cost is negligible:** ~$0.05-0.10/month per user for ALL AI features
- **6-minute Apps Script limit:** Manageable — most operations are 1-3 API calls. Batch imports can use `UrlFetchApp.fetchAll()` or time-triggered continuation

### Tier 1 — Build First (High Impact, Low-Medium Difficulty)

**1. Enhanced AI Transaction Categorization** ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
- Already partially built. Improve with fuzzy matching + corrections feedback loop.
- The #1 feature users want across ALL platforms.
- Est. 1-2 weeks

**2. Natural Language Transaction Entry** ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
- "Paid $500 rent to ABC Property" → parsed journal entry with confirmation
- Huge differentiator for non-accountants
- Est. 1-2 weeks

**3. Smart Duplicate Detection** ⭐⭐⭐⭐
- Pure Apps Script, zero API cost
- Compare date (±3 days), amount (exact), vendor (fuzzy)
- Est. 3-5 days

### Tier 2 — Build Next (High Impact, Medium Difficulty)

**4. Conversational Financial Assistant** ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
- Sidebar chat: "How much did I spend on marketing last quarter?"
- Digits charges $249+/mo for similar capability
- Est. 2-3 weeks

**5. AI-Explained Reports** ⭐⭐⭐⭐
- "Explain This Report" button → plain-English narrative of key changes
- Sends report data + prior period to LLM for variance analysis
- Est. 1-2 weeks

**6. Receipt/Invoice OCR** ⭐⭐⭐⭐
- Gemini multimodal processes photos directly
- Upload receipt → suggested journal entry
- Natural Google Drive integration
- Est. 2 weeks

### Tier 3 — Build Later

7. Automated Reconciliation Assistant (3-4 weeks)
8. Month-End Close Assistant (3-4 weeks)
9. Cash Flow Forecasting (3-4 weeks)
10. Anomaly Alerts / Financial Health Monitoring (2-3 weeks)

### LedgerWise's AI Advantage

- **Full data transparency** — it's a spreadsheet, users see everything the AI does
- **BYOK model** — bring your own API key, no intermediary, privacy-first
- **Customizable prompts** — power users can modify the Apps Script code
- **Fraction of competitor cost** — $0.05-0.10/mo vs $25-275/mo for competitors

---

## 6. UI/UX Redesign Guide {#ui-ux}

### Current UI Assessment

The current UI uses Tailwind CSS + Alpine.js + Feather Icons — a solid foundation. It's deployed as a web app (correct choice over sidebar). However, several patterns need improvement.

### Top 10 UI/UX Priorities

**1. Restructure Navigation by Workflow**

Current: flat list of 11 items. Recommended structure:

```
[Logo]
[+ New Transaction]  ← prominent button

Dashboard
Transactions
  → All Transactions
  → Bank Import
  → Mapping Rules
Accounts
  → Chart of Accounts
Reports
  → P&L
  → Balance Sheet
  → Trial Balance
  → General Ledger
Projects
Settings
```

Grouping by workflow (not accounting concept) makes the app approachable. Users spend 80% of time in Transactions.

**2. Use Tabular-Figure Typography**

All monetary columns must be right-aligned with tabular figures:
```css
.money { font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; text-align: right; }
```
This keeps decimal points aligned for easy scanning. The current Inter font supports this.

**3. Hide Double-Entry from Non-Accountants**

Frame transactions as "money in/out" with category selection. Auto-generate the journal entry. Offer "Advanced / Journal Entry" mode for accountants.

**4. Add Global Search (Ctrl+K)**

Command palette searching across transactions, accounts, contacts, reports. Add filter chips to transaction lists with preset date ranges.

**5. Design Purposeful Empty States**

Every view needs: (1) explanation of what will appear, (2) illustration/icon, (3) call-to-action button. Not just blank tables.

**6. Build a Setup Wizard**

4 steps inspired by Wave:
1. Welcome — company name, fiscal year, currency
2. Chart of Accounts — pick a template (Small Business, Freelancer, Custom)
3. Connect Bank (optional) — CSV or Plaid
4. Ready — guided tour of key areas

**7. Improve Dashboard**

- Hero metrics: Cash balance, Income this month, Expenses this month, Net profit
- Cash flow trend (line/area chart, 6-12 months)
- Action items: Uncategorized transactions count, unreconciled items
- Recent transactions

**8. Keyboard Shortcuts**

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Ctrl+K | Global search |
| Ctrl+N | New transaction |
| Ctrl+S | Save current form |
| Escape | Close modal |
| ? | Show shortcut help |

**9. Consider Switching to Lucide Icons**

1,500+ icons vs Feather's ~280. Same visual style (Lucide is Feather's successor), better coverage for financial UI (calculator, receipt, wallet, bank, credit-card, etc.).

**10. Accessibility**

- Semantic HTML tables with `<caption>`, `<th scope="col">`
- Never use color alone — always pair with text, icons, or symbols (8% of men are red-green colorblind)
- Minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio
- ARIA labels on icon-only buttons
- Visible focus indicators for keyboard navigation

### Color Palette Recommendation

| Purpose | Current | Recommended |
|---------|---------|-------------|
| Primary | Teal #008276 | Keep — works well |
| Positive/Income | Teal | Green #16a34a (standard convention) |
| Negative/Expense | Red #dc2626 | Keep |
| Warning/Pending | Amber #d97706 | Keep |
| AI-tagged | Purple #7c3aed | Keep — good differentiation |
| Background | Gray #f8fafc | Keep |
| Sidebar | Navy #02222e | Keep |

Add colorblind-safe alternates: Blue #2563eb (positive) / Orange #ea580c (negative).

### Mobile Considerations

- Tables → card layouts on narrow screens
- Sticky first column + horizontal scroll indicator
- Touch targets minimum 44×44px
- Bottom tab bar navigation
- KPIs stack vertically

### Print-Friendly Reports

- `@media print` stylesheet removing navigation and buttons
- Report header: title, date range, company name, generation timestamp
- `page-break-inside: avoid` on table rows
- Black-and-white friendly (color backed by text/symbols)

---

## 7. Strategic Positioning {#positioning}

### LedgerWise's 10 Unique Advantages

1. **Full data transparency** — users see every cell, no black box
2. **Native collaboration** — Google Sheets sharing = free multi-user access
3. **User customization** — add columns, formulas, pivot tables, conditional formatting
4. **Custom reporting power** — Sheets pivot tables beat any competitor's report builder
5. **Google ecosystem integration** — Gmail, Drive, Calendar, Forms
6. **Zero infrastructure cost** — runs entirely on Google's free tier
7. **Offline access** — via Google Sheets offline mode
8. **Deep AI integration** — Apps Script can call any AI API directly
9. **Built-in version history** — free audit trail from Google Sheets
10. **Template marketplace potential** — shareable industry-specific setups

### Positioning Statement

> **"Your books, your spreadsheet, your control — with AI that actually helps."**

Position against **Wave** (free/simple) and **GnuCash** (transparent/user-controlled) rather than trying to match QuickBooks/Xero feature-for-feature.

### Competitor Cost Comparison

| Product | Monthly Cost | AI Features |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| QuickBooks Simple Start | $38/mo | Intuit Assist |
| Xero Growing | $47/mo | JAX auto-reconcile |
| FreshBooks Plus | $43/mo | Basic |
| Wave Pro | $19/mo | Basic |
| Zoho Books Standard | $20/mo | Zia assistant |
| Digits | $249+/mo | AI-first |
| GnuCash | Free | None |
| **LedgerWise** | **Free + ~$0.10/mo API** | **Gemini-powered** |

---

## 8. Recommended Development Phases {#phases}

### Phase 0 — Security & Stability (Do First)
- [ ] Move Plaid tokens from sheet to ScriptProperties
- [ ] Fix anonymous admin fallback
- [ ] Add formula injection protection
- [ ] Fix performance: single-pass getAllBalances, PlaidService dedup Set, cache getLatestGLYear
- [ ] Unify naming: pick "LedgerWise" or "SmartLedger"
- [ ] Set up test framework (clasp + jest with GAS mocks)

### Phase 1 — Table Stakes (Must-Have Features)
- [ ] Formal bank reconciliation UI (match, clear, reconciliation report)
- [ ] Recurring transactions
- [ ] Cash Flow Statement report
- [ ] Customer/vendor management (contacts linked to transactions)
- [ ] Basic invoicing (create, send via email, track paid/unpaid)
- [ ] Accounts Receivable aging report
- [ ] Setup wizard (4-step onboarding)

### Phase 2 — AI Superpowers (Differentiation)
- [ ] Enhanced auto-categorization with fuzzy matching
- [ ] Natural language transaction entry
- [ ] Smart duplicate detection
- [ ] Conversational financial assistant (sidebar chat)
- [ ] AI-explained reports
- [ ] Receipt/invoice OCR via Gemini multimodal

### Phase 3 — UI Overhaul
- [ ] Restructure navigation by workflow
- [ ] Global search / command palette (Ctrl+K)
- [ ] Purposeful empty states for all views
- [ ] Tabular-figure typography
- [ ] Hide double-entry complexity (simple vs advanced mode)
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts
- [ ] Switch to Lucide Icons
- [ ] Accessibility improvements

### Phase 4 — Competitive Parity
- [ ] Accounts Payable / bill tracking
- [ ] Budget vs Actuals
- [ ] Multi-currency support
- [ ] Sales tax tracking
- [ ] Project profitability reports
- [ ] Scheduled report delivery via Gmail
- [ ] Print-friendly report layouts

### Phase 5 — Advanced
- [ ] AI auto-reconciliation
- [ ] Month-end close assistant
- [ ] Cash flow forecasting
- [ ] Financial health scoring
- [ ] Industry-specific templates
- [ ] Google Forms integration for expense submission

---

*This report synthesizes findings from 4 parallel research agents analyzing: code quality, competitor features, AI integration opportunities, and UI/UX best practices.*
